content: We can frame Self-Refine as a single-perspective iterative bootstrapping framework, and integrate multiple-perspective reflection mechanisms from LLM self-reflection research to create a hybrid structure where single-path iterative self-feedback is interrupted periodically by inconsistent alternative solving perspectives to break local optimization traps. This hybrid framework retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervision data, while addressing its common limitation of getting stuck in persistent blind spots that a single self-feedback loop cannot identify.
